Title: Integrative omics analysis reveals the genetic basis of fatty acid composition in Brassica napus seeds

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed oil quality trait; industrial value for erucic acid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oleic acid accumulation and reduces erucic acid accumulation in seeds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified by integrated GWAS, TWAS, seed-development transcriptomics, and POCKET prioritization at qFA.A09.1. 2. Higher expression is associated with higher C18:1 and lower C22:1 in the population. 3. Overexpression decreased seed C22:1 and increased C18:1, whereas CRISPR mutants showed the opposite effect. 4. Transcriptome and metabolite analyses suggest it affects fatty acid composition by modulating mitochondrial energy metabolism during seed development.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development stage (20, 35, 40 DAF); multi-location field evaluation across Wuhan, Ezhou, Chengdu, Hefei, Kunming, and Lanzhou
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; TWAS; haplotype-effect analysis; expression-phenotype correlation; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; seed fatty acid profiling by GC; RNA-seq of mutant seeds; subcellular localization; primary metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant overall growth differences observed in transgenic gain- or loss-of-function plants compared with controls.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: dihydroorotate dehydrogenase-like dehydrogenase involved in pyrimidine biosynthesis/energy metabolism
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: resequencing (BioProject PRJCA002835); seed transcriptome TWAS data (PRJCA002836); PYRD-related RNA-seq (PRJNA1214085); time-series transcriptome (PRJNA722877)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression at 20 and 40 DAF is positively correlated with C18:1 and negatively correlated with C22:1; increased expression in derived varieties.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase oleic acid and decrease erucic acid in rapeseed; suitable for marker-assisted haplotype selection or transgenic/edited deployment, especially combined with qFA.A08/FAE1 background.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in strongly selected locus qFA.A09.1. Proposed to affect carbon/energy metabolism and thereby influence C18:1 conversion to C18:2 and elongation to very-long-chain unsaturated fatty acids; no direct interaction with key TFs such as LEC1/LEC2/WRI1 was shown.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher at seed developmental stages linked to favorable fatty acid composition; strongest reported correlations at 20 and 40 DAF.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed oil quality trait; industrial value for erucic acid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oleic acid accumulation and reduces erucic acid accumulation in seeds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Predicted as a key candidate at qFA.A08 by integrated omics and co-expression analysis. 2. Expression is positively associated with C18:1 and negatively associated with C22:1, especially at 40 DAF. 3. Overexpression lowered C22:1 and raised C18:1, while CRISPR mutants showed the reverse phenotype. 4. Its optimal haplotype remains underutilized in breeding populations.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development stage, especially 40 DAF; multi-location field evaluation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/TWAS integration; co-expression analysis; haplotype analysis; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; seed fatty acid profiling by GC
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant overall growth differences observed in transgenic plants.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PSK1 family protein; exact functional class not further defined in the paper
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: resequencing (PRJCA002835); seed transcriptome TWAS data (PRJCA002836); time-series transcriptome (PRJNA722877)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: More significant association with fatty acid traits at 40 DAF; located in C1/C6 expression module with fatty-acid-related genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising underused target/haplotype for improving high-oleic low-erucic rapeseed; priority candidate for marker-assisted selection and gene editing.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strongest GWAS signal in qFA.A08 among candidates besides known FAE1 background; optimal haplotype not widely adopted across regions or breeding periods.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed developmental expression pattern similar to several fatty acid synthesis-related genes; stronger significance at 40 DAF.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed oil quality trait; industrial value for erucic acid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oleic acid accumulation and reduces erucic acid accumulation in seeds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a top candidate near qFA.A08 through POCKET scoring and integrated omics. 2. Shows the strongest expression correlation with higher C18:1 and lower C22:1 among tested candidates. 3. CRISPR mutants increased C22:1 and decreased C18:1. 4. Favorable haplotype frequency increased during breeding and is now predominant in many accessions.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development stage (20 and 40 DAF); multi-location field evaluation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/TWAS integration; POCKET prioritization; expression-phenotype correlation; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; seed fatty acid profiling by GC; haplotype frequency analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant overall growth differences observed in mutants.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: resequencing (PRJCA002835); seed transcriptome TWAS data (PRJCA002836); time-series transcriptome (PRJNA722877)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression strongly correlated with C18:1 positively and C22:1 negatively at both 20 and 40 DAF.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable allele/haplotype for high-oleic low-erucic breeding; suitable for editing or haplotype selection.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains a reported frameshift mutation in exon potentially altering protein function; despite chromatin-related annotation, the paper does not define direct downstream metabolic target genes, so treated here as common gene rather than confirmed regulator.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongest trait-expression correlation among six candidates at 20 and 40 DAF.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed oil quality trait; industrial value for erucic acid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oleic acid accumulation and reduces erucic acid accumulation in seeds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified within qFA.C02 and co-expression module C3 as a candidate associated with fatty acid composition. 2. Haplotypes significantly affect C18:1 and C22:1 when qFA.A08 is fixed. 3. CRISPR mutants increased C22:1 and decreased C18:1. 4. Favorable haplotype frequency increased during breeding, indicating utility in improvement.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development stage, especially 20 DAF-related correlation; multi-location field evaluation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/TWAS integration; co-expression analysis; haplotype analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; seed fatty acid profiling by GC
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant overall growth differences observed in mutants.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: resequencing (PRJCA002835); seed transcriptome TWAS data (PRJCA002836); time-series transcriptome (PRJNA722877)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern resembles seed coat development and lipid-synthesis genes such as TT9, LPAAT, and DGD2; higher relevance at 20 DAF.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding higher oleic and lower erucic acid rapeseed through haplotype selection or gene editing.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper links gene expression module to both seed coat development and lipid synthesis, but direct biochemical mechanism is not resolved.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with C3 expression module; positive relation to favorable fatty acid composition.
